channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: 3D Realms Co-Founder Believes Layoffs Have Begun At Zenimax Studios
item.content:encoded:
Days after the reports began regarding other Xbox studios, one of its biggest first-party developers is also going to look drastically different. According to 3D Realms and Apogee co-founder George Broussard, layoffs have begun at Zenimax Studios.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news from Google Play or the App Store.

Broussard made the claims in a post on social media on Wednesday.

“Getting word that layoffs at Zenimax has begun,” he said. “Heard rumblings recently that the cuts may be much deeper than you expect.”

Rumors began to circulate earlier this week that Xbox was looking to let go of those employees within the studio who aren’t working on Fallout or Elder Scrolls. It’s something that Broussard said he was told as well.

As always, these are just rumors at the moment. Until something is officially announced, or Insider Gaming can confirm the details with our own sources, please take the information with a grain of salt.

Xbox hasn’t responded to requests for comment on the latest claims.

Dark days ahead for Xbox

In addition to the latest rumors surrounding Zenimax, Xbox is set to shut down Compulsion Games, Ninja Theory, and Double Fine. Management at the three studios are in negotiations to find a way to keep the studios alive as independent entities.

  • Some Good News | Insider Gaming Showcase Participant Smash ‘N Grab Nominated For Award

Another studio Insider Gaming has learned is at risk is Arkane Lyon. It’s worth noting, however, that it’s unclear if a definitive decision has been made.

What do you make of the claims that layoffs are happening at Zenimax? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the latest major player milestone Peak surpassed as it hits its first anniversary. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 is Getting a Free Suit from ‘Brand New Day’
item.content:encoded:
Insomniac has announced that players will receive a free suit in Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, inspired by the upcoming movie Spider-Man: Brand New Day.

In 2024, Insomniac confirmed that they do not plan to release any additional story content for Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, unlike their previous title. However, they have kept players engaged with a variety of cosmetics and game improvements. There is a new announcement, it won’t be any story DLC, but players can enjoy a new suit introduced just in time for the upcoming movie.

Before we get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Marvel’s Spider-Man 2’s Free Brand New Day Suit is Called ‘Fresh Start’

The developers announced the release of a new free suit for Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, inspired by Spider-Man: Brand New Day, through a post on their official X account (formerly Twitter). The new suit is called “Fresh Start” and will be available for PS5 and PC players through an in-game update scheduled to arrive on July 28, just days before the release of the upcoming movie. Tom Holland is seen wearing this suit in the movie’s latest trailer.

More Insider Gaming News: Destiny 2 Final Update’s Success Hasn’t ‘Moved the Needle’ at Bungie

Spider-Man: Brand New Day is set to release on July 31 and introduces players to a brand-new story featuring Peter Parker. Following the events of the previous film, everyone has forgotten who Spider-Man and Peter Parker are, leaving the friendly neighborhood superhero feeling isolated. To make matters worse, he seems to be losing control of his powers.

Fans will also see the arrival of characters like Bruce Banner and Frank Castle as they come to Peter’s aid in the face of a new threat. With this upcoming update, players will have the opportunity to suit up like the character in Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, allowing them to experience what it feels like to be in Peter’s shoes as he navigates this latest adventure.

What are your thoughts on Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 getting a free suit inspired by Brand New Day? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Black Ops and Black Ops 2 ports coming to PlayStation in July. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 Ports Are Coming to PlayStation in July
item.content:encoded:
It has been announced that both Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 will be getting ports for the PlayStation 4 and 5 this July.

Before you continue reading, though, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the best way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Black Ops Ports on PlayStation

As of the time of writing, there’s been no official confirmation on whether the Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 ports will be making their way to Xbox or PC.

The news was first reported by the official Treyarch Call of Duty account, which tweeted on X, “It’s official: the original Black Ops and Black Ops 2 are being ported to PlayStation in July, courtesy of our partners at Iron Galaxy.”

RELATED – Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Release, Platforms, Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status

Call of Duty: Black Ops 2 was first released in November 2010, with the sub-series’ second installment releasing in 2012. Both games, which are regarded as some of the best in the franchise, had only been available on their original platform releases until now, of course, in July.

It’s worth noting that these are just ports of the original games, and won’t be remasters or remakes, and it’s currently unknown how Activision plans to handle the multiple servers and capabilities (which are quite outdated).

But as always, we’ll be sure to keep our readers updated as we start to learn more about the Black Ops ports.

Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord what you think of Black Ops 1 and 2 ports coming to PlayStation.

Read about Marathon players accusing Bungie of ‘Killing the Game’ with a Season 2 update for more news, and sign up for our free Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: Destiny 2 Final Update’s Success Hasn’t ‘Moved the Needle’ at Bungie
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has confirmed that all the players gathering for Destiny 2’s final update haven’t helped improve things at Bungie, with significant layoffs on the way.

Fans of Bungie’s highly popular series have been in a bad state for the past couple of weeks, but the support towards the game has been enormous at the same time. So much so that the players were able to break servers and even beat Marathon’s peak player count for the final update. However, all the success hasn’t helped much internally.

Before we get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Has Destiny 2’s Final Update Helped Improve Things at Bungie? – ‘No’

In his latest YouTube video, Paul Tassi discussed the current state of Destiny 2 and Bungie following the final update. He first talked about the recent reports of Bungie laying off approximately 400 staff members during the summer layoffs. While he couldn’t confirm the exact number, he did mention that significant layoffs were expected. Tassi emphasized that this situation was inevitable if the developer chose to shut down a game with half its workforce dedicated to it.

Tassi also inquired with his internal sources about whether the highly successful final update for Destiny 2 had improved the situation at Bungie. Given that the game ranked third on the Steam revenue charts over the past week, he wondered whether this had made any difference. He received a straightforward one-word response: “No.”

  • More Insider Gaming News: Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Director Reveals Reasoning Behind New Title

The insider also added that the game is receiving a ‘final hotfix’, which means there will be no ongoing support for the title. The only time a team will intervene is if there is something game-breaking. “They are not even keeping a small team around to do like the bare bones of what it would require to keep this game from breaking,” said Tassi.

What are your thoughts on Destiny 2’s final update not moving the needle at Bungie? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Marathon players accusing Bungie of ‘killing the game’ with Season 2. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: “We believe that he can be the difference-maker for us” – NiKo on playing with karrigan in the Lanxess
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to legendary Falcons rifler, Nikola ‘NiKo’ Kovač, before Team Falcons’ IEM Cologne 2026 Major quarter-final against Team Vitality.

The veteran star reflected on Falcons’ victory over NAVI, the team’s growing understanding under Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen, and his own new responsibilities in the roster.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Insider Gaming interview with Niko at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

You’re in the quarter-finals. You have some good memories in the Lanxess, but some bad memories as well. How are you feeling going into the playoffs?

Feeling very good, really happy with the way we won versus NAVI. Super excited to be back in Lanxess again. It’s a special place for CS and the community as well.

Now, this time around, it’s also the Major, so it’s going to be even more special to play in the arena. Just looking forward to the game.

So you mentioned there, you were pleased with how you beat NAVI. What aspects of that win were meaningful to you?

I would say that we had a very strong mental state against them. We were down on Anubis in the second half, 11-6 or 11-7. We managed to come back, you could even argue that we could have won without overtime, but we are still really happy with the way we approached overtime, and we managed to close it out.

Everyone had his own moments. You know, TeSeS has been on fire versus NAVI. It was really nice to see him playing the way he did.

He just showed up on the day, and it seemed that with the opponents getting better, we also stepped up our game a little bit as well, so that was very important to see.

And yeah, beating Na’Vi in such a close fashion was a good confidence boost for the playoffs.

It feels like the T-sides are becoming more online. How are you feeling in the server? Is there more connective tissue now between the players on the T-side?

Yeah, I mean, Finn is a very special IGL and one of the most experienced players out there with a really good track record as well. I think what he brings the most to the T-side is versatility, I would say.

We are very, very versatile on the T-side. The rounds can be very open or can also be very structured. So we have, I think, a pretty good balance of when we need to play together as a team and when some players need to do something individually as well.

I think he’s obviously still learning about the players on the team because he has just joined one month ago, basically. But so far, it has been very good working with him. He brings a lot of new ideas, just a different style, a different identity to the team as well.

It’s been nice working with him so far.

  • Related: Vitality’s IGL apEX interviewed at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

Last time you played with karrigan, he didn’t have that reputation of having an arena buff. Now that the roster is feeling more cohesive, do you think that experience is going to be valuable when it comes to those big arena moments?

Definitely. That’s definitely one of the reasons why we decided to bring such an experience. The player has already won a lot of stuff. We believe that he can be the thing that can take us to the next level.

You know, Finn is always a special player when it comes to the stage matches. He’s a showman, and he brings something special out of the players and also the crowd.

So, this is going to be the first time that we share like a really big stage together, you know, even though we have played in Astana and Shanghai. But this time around, the Lanxess is very special.

It’s going to be nice to be on the stage with him, and we believe that he can be the difference-maker for us when it comes to these games.

We know you’ve been calling CT-sides for a while. In terms of the T-side, would you give that kind of secondary voice in the mid-round?

Yeah. As you mentioned, I’ve been already calling the CT-side, even though the CT side is kind of a shared responsibility. Everyone has to contribute to the team.

For the T side, I would say that I have more space now with Finn because his way of calling is a bit more open, so I think he needs players to give more ideas and more directions.

I believe that this is where I can kind of chime in and help him a lot. So in that sense, my role is a bit different now that my voice is a bit more activated in the rounds.

While with Damjan [kyxsan], I was a bit more quiet because he had a different way of calling, which was also fine. And yeah. When it comes to calling, that’s pretty much it.

Then I’m just, I’m a bit more focused on lurking as well because Finn doesn’t like to finish rounds with five people on the one site. So I’m lurking a bit more as well than I used to, and that’s pretty much it.

How are you finding those lurking positions?

I mean, I think it’s been up and down. There are games or events where I feel really good, and then there are some games where I can’t do much for some reason. So I’m still finding myself, I would say.

I think that I have improved my game a little bit since Finn joined. I think I’m a bit more stable, but it has been a difficult transition in these roles, but I’m trying to improve.

I want to be a stable player for the team. I want to, I try to focus on getting better in clutches as well, because this is something that we need on the team. So far, at least this event has been working pretty good and hopefully I can keep it up.

End of interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see Big John’s participation at Valorant Masters London, bosh! And sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
